claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have a child or children that you're responsible for?** If yes, could you please tell me:
- How many children you have
- Their ages (or dates of birth)
- Their names (so I can keep track of each child's eligibility)

This will help me understand your situation and guide you through the eligibility assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do have a child. I have one child - Alex, who is 17 years old and lives at home with me. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can help you assess eligibility for Alex.

Since Alex is 17 years old, I need to ask some important follow-up questions:

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?**

This is important because for young people aged 16 or 17, Child Benefit can continue if they're in approved education or training. This typically includes:
- Full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels or equivalent)
- Approved training programs

Could you let me know if Alex is in any form of education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in education or training at the moment. They left school and aren't studying anything right now.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that information about Alex.

Based on what you've told me, I now have enough information to assess eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Let me explain the situation:**

For children aged 16 or 17, Child Benefit can only continue if they are in approved education or training. Since Alex is 17 and is not currently in education or training, you would not be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Child Benefit eligibility for young people aged 16-19 depends on them being enrolled in approved education or training. Once they leave education or training (and don't continue to another approved course), entitlement to Child Benefit stops.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**However, please note:** If Alex does decide to return to education or start an approved training program in the future, you may be able to claim Child Benefit again at that point, as long as they're under 20 when they start the course.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit or your situation?

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old and not in approved education or training. Under UK Child Benefit rules, for young people aged 16-19, Child Benefit can only be claimed if they are in approved education or training. Since Alex has left school and is not currently studying or in any training program, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex, aged 17, who is not in education or training. Child Benefit eligibility for 16-17 year olds requires them to be in approved education or training, which Alex is not. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit.

Since Alex has left school and is not currently studying or in any training program, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
